017package org.apache.juneau.config.internal;
018
019import java.io.*;
020import java.util.*;
021
022import org.apache.juneau.config.format.*;
023import org.apache.juneau.config.store.*;
024
025/**
026 * Merges a base configuration with one or more profile overlays into a single internal-INI string.
027 *
028 * <p>
029 * This is the engine behind config <b>profiles</b> ({@code <name>-<profile>.cfg}/{@code .yml} overlays activated via
030 * {@code juneau.profiles.active}).  The base config is the foundation; each active profile's entries are layered on
031 * top in activation order so that:
032 * <ul>
033 *    <li><b>profile wins over base</b> for any section/key the profile redefines, and
034 *    <li><b>last-active-profile wins</b> when multiple active profiles redefine the same section/key.
035 * </ul>
036 *
037 * <p>
038 * Each input (base + profiles) is parsed through its own {@link ConfigFormat} (so a YAML profile is converted to the
039 * internal INI form like any other config), then the parsed maps are overlaid entry-by-entry and re-emitted as a
040 * single internal-INI string via {@link ConfigMap#asIniString()}.  Feeding that merged string back through the normal
041 * {@link ConfigMap} parse keeps format handling, variable resolution, and change-listener wiring identical to a
042 * single-file config &mdash; profiles are transparent to everything downstream of the store.
043 *
044 * @since 10.0.0
045 */
046public final class ProfileMerge {
047
048   private ProfileMerge() {}
049
050   /**
051    * Merges base + profile contents into one internal-INI string (profile-wins, last-active-profile-wins).
052    *
053    * @param store The store the throwaway parse maps are associated with (used only for parse context; never written).
054    * @param baseName The base config name (for parse diagnostics).
055    * @param baseContents The raw base config contents (format-native).
056    * @param profiles Ordered profile overlays (activation order); each is a raw format-native contents string.  May be empty.
057    * @param format The config format used to parse every input.
058    * @return The merged contents as an internal-INI string.
059    * @throws IOException If any input fails to parse.
060    */
061   public static String merge(ConfigStore store, String baseName, String baseContents, List<String> profiles, ConfigFormat format) throws IOException {
062      var f = format == null ? IniConfigFormat.INSTANCE : format;
063      // Parse the base into a throwaway map; this becomes the accumulator we overlay onto.
064      var merged = new ConfigMap(store, baseName, baseContents == null ? "" : baseContents, f);
065      for (var p : profiles) {
066         if (p == null || p.isBlank())
067            continue;
068         var overlay = new ConfigMap(store, baseName, p, f);
069         applyOverlay(merged, overlay);
070      }
071      return merged.asIniString();
072   }
073
074   /**
075    * Overlays every section/key entry from {@code overlay} onto {@code base} (overlay wins).
076    */
077   private static void applyOverlay(ConfigMap base, ConfigMap overlay) {
078      for (var section : overlay.getSections()) {
079         // Ensure the section exists on the base (preserving the overlay's section pre-lines when it is new).
080         if (! base.hasSection(section))
081            base.setSection(section, overlay.getPreLines(section));
082         for (var key : overlay.getKeys(section)) {
083            var e = overlay.getEntry(section, key);
084            if (e != null)  // HTT: getKeys() only returns keys with entries, so getEntry is never null here.
085               base.setEntry(section, key, e.getValue(), e.getModifiers(), e.getComment(), e.getPreLines());
086         }
087      }
088   }
089}
